DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH DISCOVERY REQUESTS; FOR IMPOSITION OF FURTHER MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST VEREXO AND ITS COUNSEL; REQUEST FOR ISSUANCE OF O S C REGARDING CONTEMPT - HAVING READ AND CONSIDERED THE MOTION, INCLUDING THE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, AND ALL TIMELY SUBMITTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE, AND GOOD CAUSE APPEARING THEREFOR, IT IS ORDERED THAT DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT ZINIO, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. WITHIN TEN DAYS OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER, DEBTOR VEREXO, INC. SHALL SERVE COMPLETE, VERIFIED DISCOVERY RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, COMPLETE PRODUCTION OF ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS, AND PAY $1,872 IN ATTORNEYS' FEES AND $60 IN COURT COSTS TO THE MOVING PARTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: NAOMI GRAY; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Motion To Compel Compliance With Discovery Requests; For Imposition Of Further Monetary Sanctions Against Verexo And Its Counsel; Request For Issuance Of Osc Regarding Contempt; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES SMITH, TEL. NO. 1-415-273-7354 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC's Motion To Compel Compliance With Discovery Requests; For Imposition Of Further Monetary Sanctions Against Verexo And Its Counsel; Request For Issuance Of O S C Regarding Contempt - Having read and considered the motion, including the memorandum of points and authorities, and all timely submitted admissible evidence, and good cause appearing therefor, IT IS ORDERED THAT Defendant and Cross-Complainant Zinio, LLC's Motion to Compel is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Within ten days of entry of the Court's Order, Debtor Verexo, Inc. shall serve complete, verified discovery responses, without objections, complete production of all responsive documents, and pay $1,872 in attorneys' fees and $60 in court costs to the moving party. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Gray; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt

Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Text Ruling

Second Omnibus Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Darius Samerotte of Kilpatrick Townsend & Stockton LLP telephone number 415-273-4365 for Defendant.

No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. Counsel for the Defendant stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC'S Second Omnibus Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of Documents; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. Granted is the motion to compel responses to written discovery described in its Notice of Motion within twenty days of service of this Order with Notice of Entry. Also granted is deeming defendant's request for admissions (second set) to be admitted. Sanctions are awarded in the sum of $1,556.00. Denied is the request for any future relief in the nature of evidentiary or terminating sanctions conditioned on compliance with the Order entered herein. A new and separate motion for that type of relief is required. Defendant shall prepare a proposed Order which specifically references the precise discovery to which written responses are due. Order signed and modified.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

Omnibus Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of All Responsive Documents; To Compel Attendance At Depositions; To Bar Evidence Of Claimed Account; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Or Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DARIUS SAMEROTTE OF THE KILPATRICK TOWNSEND LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 576-0200 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ZINIO, LLC'S Omnibus Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery; To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted; To Compel Production Of All Responsive Documents; To Compel Attendance At Depositions; To Bar Evidence Of Claimed Account; And Request For Issue, Evidentiary, And Or Terminating Sanctions (part 1 of 2) - No Opposition was received to the pending omnibus motion. The omnibus motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows:
1. Plaintiff has failed to timely respond to the written discovery that are the subject of this motion. Plaintiff is deemed to have waived all objections to the written discovery and plaintiff is ordered to provide substantive written responses, without any objections, to moving party Zinio's Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Requests for Documents, within ten days from notice of this Order;
2. Plaintiff is ordered to produce any and all responsive documents, without withholding any responsive documents based upon any objection whatsoever, within ten days from notice of this Order;
3. Zinio's Requests for Admissions are hereby deemed admitted;
4. Verexo, Inc. is ordered to produce the Person Most Qualified to testify on its behalf
as to the matters listed in Zinio's Notice of Deposition dated January 29, 2015
(attached as Exhibit H to the Declaration of James Smith in Support of the Motion)
on a business day to be unilaterally designated by Zinio for a date no sooner than
three weeks after the date of notice of this Order; (see part 2) =(302/JPT)
5. Plaintiff is ordered to provide substantive written responses to Zinio's Bill of
Particulars, without objections, within ten days from notice of this Order and if
Plaintiff fails to timely do so then plaintiff shall be deemed precluded from giving evidence in support of the account on which the plaintiff's second cause of action is
based, pursuant to C.C.P. 454;
6. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,228.00 is awarded to Zinio against plaintiff for plaintiff's failures to respond and abuse of the discovery process;
7. Zinio's request to set a hearing on an Order To Show Cause to strike plaintiff's Complaint and/or answer to Zinio's Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Zinio shall prepare a formal Order. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.
